quotes.verbatim_quote: Obviously, if turnarounds are common, you will need to know how to improvise your way through them. Create licks that you know will work over various turnarounds. This will help you make melodically smoother transitions back to the beginnings of your tunes. Study the following turnarounds going to C. Memorize and transpose the ones you like.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Core practice rule for turnaround improvisation: learn, memorize, transpose licks.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Here is a chord solo loosely based on the changes of the song Misty. This arrangement incorporates many of the concepts that have been covered in the "A" lessons of this book. Use it to reinforce your understanding of what you have learned.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Frames chord solo as synthesis of book's A-lesson chord-melody concepts.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Here is a single note solo also loosely based on the chord changes of Misty. This solo incorporates many of the concepts that have been covered in the "B" lessons of this book. Use it to solidify what you have learned.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Frames single-note solo as synthesis of book's B-lesson improvisation concepts.
